questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Two moles of hydrogen are mixed with $n$ moles of helium. The root mean square speed of gas molecules in the mixture is $\sqrt{2}$ times the speed of sound in the mixture. Then $n$ is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
A horizontal insulated cylinder of volume $V$ is divided into four identical compartments by stationary semi-permeable thin partitions as shown. The four compartments from left are initially filled with 28 g helium, 160 g oxygen, 28 g nitrogen and 20 g hydrogen respectively. The left partition lets through hydrogen, nitrogen and helium while the right partition lets through hydrogen only. The middle partition lets through hydrogen and nitrogen both. The temperature $T$ inside the entire cylinder is maintained constant. After the system is set in equilibrium,
\begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|}\hline He & \mathrm{O}_2 & \mathrm{~N}_2 & \mathrm{H}_2 \\\hline\end{array}
options: pressure of helium is $\frac{14 R T}{V}$.
pressure of oxygen is $\frac{20 R T}{V}$.
pressure of nitrogen is $\frac{4 R T}{3 V}$.
pressure of hydrogen is $\frac{10 R T}{V}$.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: The average translational kinetic energy of oxygen ( $\mathrm{M}=32$ ) molecules at a certain temperature is 0.048 eV . The translational kinetic energy of nitrogen ( $M=28$ ) molecules at the same temperature is (consider the two gases to be ideal)
